machine_spec: Apple M3 Pro / Mac15,7, 12 CPU core (6P+6E), 18 GPU core, 36 GB unified, macOS 26.5, Metal 4
goal: 3x better core KPIs for the Mac Qwen3.6-27B (decode/prefill tok/s)

model.name: Qwen3.6-27B
model.arch: Gated-DeltaNet hybrid (cfg.IsQwen35Hybrid, 48 GDN layers + periodic full-attention)

flags: Write three sentences about why the ocean is important.
command.backend_banner: fak in-kernel Gated-DeltaNet (resident Q4_K decode + Q8 fallback, cached) [Metal q4_k prefill]

clean_llama_stopped.note: launchd com.fak.qwen36-model booted out for a clean 36 GB GPU; restored via EXIT trap

contended_llama_up.note: both 27B models resident, swap thrash — not a fak number, kept for contrast

interpretation.status: diagnosis: Metal Q4_K path correct but far below the bar
interpretation.decode_verdict: 1.2 tok/s clean = 0.16x of the 3x goal and 0.16x of llama.cpp-Metal; kernels are bit-correct, the wall is ~336 separate per-token command-buffer GEMVs at ~360us fixed launch overhead each, plus low in-kernel utilization (GEMV ~21% BW, GEMM ~5% FLOP)
interpretation.primary_lever: one-command-buffer-per-token GPU-resident decode forward (decode twin of metalgemm.Prefill/forward.m mg_prefill) — keep the activation on-GPU across all matmuls, read GPU-resident KV, submit one command buffer/token; GDN recurrent scan must run on-GPU or tight-hybrid
interpretation.secondary_lever: kernel-efficiency pass on internal/metalgemm/q4k.m (threadgroup/grid sizing, vectorized coalesced dequant, simdgroup_matrix GEMM tile)
